title: Flourishing Under Threat: Applying the NARMA Model to Institutional Bullying Redress Frameworks
author: Centre for Wellbeing and Flourishing | DSA | UCT
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Policies designed to address workplace bullying are often evaluated in terms of their legal structure, procedural fairness, and institutional compliance. However, less attention is paid to how these policies interact with the inner psychological state of individuals navigating harm. This paper introduces the NARMA Model of Languishing—Negativity, Apathy, Restriction, Meaninglessness, and Adversity—as a conceptual lens to explore the cognitive and emotional terrain faced by complainants. Using the University of Cape Town’s 2021 Policy Addressing Bullying as a structural case study, this paper analyzes how each component of NARMA may be inadvertently activated during the redress journey. The argument advanced is not that policy fails at the level of protocol, but that it often lacks the psychological scaffolding required to sustain human dignity and momentum. This framework provides a vocabulary for describing the internal resistance to flourishing that formal procedures may unintentionally provoke.
